bug_report.summary: Document statistics (words/characters) change drastically after fix when Pandoc attribute block is present
steps_to_reproduce: Type a heading with a Pandoc attribute block containing quoted values, e.g. `# Title {style="font-family: 'Noto Serif'; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.05em;"}`.
Observe the word/character counters in the UI (top-right indicator and/or editor status bar).
expected_behaviors: Word/character counts should remain consistent with the visible source text (or at minimum should not change unexpectedly compared to previous behavior for the same typed content).
actual_behaviors: For the same visible content, the counters differ significantly between builds: before-change shows higher counts (e.g., 18 words / 115 characters) while after-change shows much lower counts (e.g., 7 words / 38 characters), suggesting the attribute block is being excluded from stats after the change.

bug_report.summary: Stray vertical line/artifact appears near right-side pane divider after change
steps_to_reproduce: Open Zettlr to the main editor window with split panes visible.
Open (or create) any document so the right-side panes are shown.
Look near the right editor/preview area around the horizontal pane divider (approx. x=894–904, y=534–562).
expected_behaviors: Pane dividers and adjacent UI should render cleanly without extra stray marks/lines.
actual_behaviors: A small vertical line/strip renders differently (appears as an artifact/extra mark) in the divider area (bbox ~[894,534,904,562]).

bug_report.summary: Pandoc image attributes render as stray literal text in preview (e.g., {width="10" height="25"})
steps_to_reproduce: Type the following on its own line: ![Pixel](test.png){width="10" height="25"}
expected_behaviors: The attribute block should be parsed and applied to the image (or at minimum not rendered as visible text).
No trailing `{width="10" height="25"}` text should appear in the preview.
actual_behaviors: The preview renders the attribute block `{width="10" height="25"}` as visible text beneath the image placeholder ("Image not found: test.png").

bug_report.summary: Creating a new Markdown file opens/focuses the file in a different pane (left editor vs lower-right pane)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the app with split view enabled (multiple panes visible).
Click “Save” in the file dialog to create the new document.
expected_behaviors: The newly created blank Markdown document becomes the active document in the main editor pane (consistent behavior; matches prior build).
Focus/active tab should switch to the new file in a predictable pane.
actual_behaviors: Before change: the new file becomes the active tab in the left editor and shows as a blank document (0 words / 0 characters).
After change: the left editor stays on “Welcome to Zettlr!” content, while the new blank file opens as a tab in the lower-right pane instead (showing 0 words / 0 characters there).
